input.headline: Wales’ route to World Cup 2026 in full after play-off draw

raw.headline: Wales’ route to World Cup 2026 in full after play-off draw

winner: Wales has secured their spot at the 2026 World Cup with such confidence that they’ve already booked a one-way ticket to... America’s wallet.

candidates.text: Wales' World Cup qualification path is now complete, which is great news for everyone involved—except perhaps the teams they drew last, who are currently considering a career change.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the teams 'considering a career change' adds humor, concise and specific to the context."}

candidates.text: Wales has secured their spot at the 2026 World Cup with such confidence that they’ve already booked a one-way ticket to... America’s wallet.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a surprising twist linking Wales' World Cup qualification to America's wallet, but the punchline could be more specific and tighter for maximum impact."}

candidates.text: Wales is so excited about reaching the World Cup that they've already started drafting their national anthem—with extra verses for every time someone says "draw.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7, "reason": "The joke is clever and specific, playing on the word 'draw' in both the play-off context and the national anthem. However, it's slightly forced and lacks a punchline that fully lands."}

candidates.text: The Welsh football team is celebrating their qualification for the World Cup—because they finally found a penalty area large enough for all their goalposts.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and has a mild surprise element, but the humor is weak and relies on a forced pun that doesn't strongly connect to the headline or the context of Wales qualifying for the World Cup."}

candidates.text: Wales qualified for the World Cup, which is great—now they just have to figure out how to lose.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat funny but relies on a stereotype about Wales' performance in tournaments, which may not be universally applicable or well-received."}
